full_prompt: Design a portable bubble CPAP (continuous positive airway pressure) device chassis targeting neonatal respiratory distress syndrome in LMIC clinical settings. The system must deliver continuous positive airway pressure in the 3-8 cmH2O range (294-785 Pa) at a neonatal flow of 5-10 L/min of blended air/oxygen mix, using the classic bubble-CPAP principle where expiratory limb is submerged in a water column to set pressure. The device chassis is a 250 mm x 180 mm x 220 mm polypropylene injection-molded enclosure housing an oil-free diaphragm blower (25 W electrical, 12 L/min max flow), a venturi oxygen blender, a humidifier chamber, and a pressure-relief valve, powered by a 12 VDC supply or a 12 V 5 Ah battery. BOM cost target is under $250 per unit. Pass/fail criteria: (R1) the bubble column must produce a CPAP pressure within +/- 0.5 cmH2O of the target 5 cmH2O at 8 L/min flow per WHO Technical Specification ref WHO/MCA/18.12, (R2) chassis max von Mises stress under a 1 m drop test (equivalent quasi-static 120 N impulse on the enclosure) must not exceed 20 MPa (PP yield 30 / SF 1.5), (R3) humidifier reservoir must maintain inspired gas temperature between 34-37 C with a 50 W heater at ambient 25 C in steady state, (R4) electrical power draw at 8 L/min, 5 cmH2O must not exceed 25 W to keep 5 Ah battery endurance at least 2 hours, and (R5) total device mass must not exceed 2.5 kg for WHO portable-device portability target. Thermal analysis is required for R3. Vibration, fluid CFD, and radiation analyses are not required; bubble-column pressure uses static column hydrostatics. Deliver a STEP AP242 assembly with chassis, blower, humidifier, valves, and battery bay as sub-bodies plus a verification report listing delivered CPAP pressure, chassis stress, humidifier steady-state temperature, electrical draw, and total mass.
short_prompt:
Design a portable bubble-CPAP chassis for neonatal respiratory distress in LMIC clinical settings. The device delivers 3-8 cmH2O continuous positive airway pressure at 5-10 L/min blended air/O2 using the classic bubble-column pressure-setting scheme, housed in a polypropylene injection-molded enclosure on the order of 250 x 180 x 220 mm containing an oil-free diaphragm blower, venturi O2 blender, heated humidifier, and pressure-relief valve, powered by 12 VDC or a 12 V 5 Ah battery (60 Wh). BOM target under $250/unit.

Design to the WHO Technical Specification for bubble CPAP (WHO/MCA/18.12). Chassis sits on a table with a grip handle.

Load cases.
- LC1 CPAP setpoint: 5 cmH2O +/- 0.5 at 8 L/min (bubble-column hydrostatic).
- LC2 1 m drop equivalent quasi-static: 120 N on the enclosure.
- LC3 humidifier thermal steady state: 50 W heater against 25 C ambient, target inspired-gas 34-37 C.
- LC4 electrical draw endurance: 25 W max continuous draw to reach 2 h on 60 Wh battery.

Total device mass is a WHO portability constraint.

requires_non_fea_solver.reason: Bubble-CPAP pressure is the static hydrostatic head of the submerged expiratory limb (rho g h), not FEA.

prompt.title: Portable bubble CPAP chassis for LMIC neonatal use
prompt.brief: Design a 250 x 180 x 220 mm PP chassis for a portable bubble CPAP delivering 3-8 cmH2O at 5-10 L/min, under $250 BOM, with 50 W humidifier holding 34-37 C and 25 W max draw for 2 h on 5 Ah battery.

boundary_conditions.chassis: placed on table, grip handle on top

objective.primary: meet WHO CPAP accuracy + thermal band

recommended_fea_setup.element_type: shell for chassis, solid for humidifier

exclusions_explained.vibration_analysis: Blower vibration out of scope for this chassis brief.
exclusions_explained.fluid_analysis: Bubble-column pressure is hydrostatic; no CFD needed for this brief.

out_of_scope_but_real_world_needed: Full ISO 80601-2-79 neonatal ventilator standard (bubble CPAP is outside scope of that standard)

eval_coverage: LMIC low-cost + portable medical device envelope
